prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?
context: iter 3 broadens path_patterns to /chatglm/ prefix (covers backend-api + mainchat-api, excludes root HTML page-load). Test on test PC.
observation: Chrome opened fresh to https://chatglm.cn/main/alltoolsdetail?lang=zh. Sidebar showed historical conversations from a previous authenticated session (including '내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234...' entry from iter 2 #952). Page initially displayed home/landing view with placeholder '和我聊聊天吧' in input area and welcome banner '今天的难题，交给我试试看吧'. F12 + Ctrl+F8 (Deactivate breakpoints) applied. First prompt submission attempt via clipboard paste + Enter at chat-input area coords (1536/2, 780) did NOT register (page remained on home view, no user bubble rendered). Second attempt after clicking '新对话' (new conversation) sidebar item triggered ChatGLM login dialog — '微信扫码登录' (WeChat QR) on left and '手机号登录' (phone-number login) on right. Session is unauthenticated for new submissions despite sidebar showing prior chat history (likely cached client-side list; server requires fresh auth for write operations). 45s wait completed before login dialog observed. Could not validate APF block behavior because no chat request was actually transmitted.
anti_debugger_note: Per iter 2 lesson: site fires VM debugger statements when DevTools is open; Ctrl+F8 Deactivate-breakpoints toggle was applied immediately after F12.
auth_state: logged_out — sidebar history visible (cached) but submission gated by login dialog
screenshots: results/954_screenshots/chatglm_iter3_home_view.png — initial home view after URL load (sidebar shows prior chats, input empty with placeholder)
results/954_screenshots/chatglm_iter3_login_dialog.png — login dialog (微信扫码登录 / 手机号登录) appearing when attempting to use new conversation
diagnosis: Iter 3 path_patterns broadening to /chatglm/ prefix could not be validated on this run because the test PC's ChatGLM session is no longer authenticated. The submission path was blocked at the frontend by the login dialog before any /chatglm/* network request could be issued. Sidebar entries persisted client-side, masking the auth state during initial inspection. To validate iter 3 the test PC must be re-logged-in to chatglm.cn (likely via WeChat QR on user's phone, or SMS code) before the request is re-issued. Independent of iter 3 correctness — this run does not produce a verdict on path_patterns coverage.
next_iter_hint: Re-authenticate ChatGLM on test PC (manual step — WeChat QR or SMS), then re-run iter 3 with same broadened /chatglm/ prefix. If auth persists, expect either (a) warning bubble with '차단' rendered = OK, or (b) PII analysis still streamed = path still misses real endpoint (need new HAR). Recommend pre-flight auth check in test-pc-worker for chatglm.
